forecast-period.text: A high pressure system centred over the Bight will move slowly eastwards during Sunday and Monday. A cold front will move across southern coastal districts on Tuesday then another high will move across waters to the south mid to late next week.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones.

Isolated light showers mostly about southern coastal districts. Isolated light showers about the northern Flinders Ranges. Areas of morning fog inland and patchy frost about parts of the Agricultural Area. Maximum temperatures below average. Light winds.

Isolated showers about the Southern Agricultural Area, more frequent about windward coasts and ranges. A chance of isolated light showers about the south of the pastoral districts and in the west. Southwest to southerly winds in the north but south to southeasterly in the south.

Isolated light showers possible near southern coasts. Areas of morning fog inland and patchy frost about parts of the Agricultural Area, Flinders district and far south of the pastoral districts. Maximum temperatures below average. Light winds tending west to northwesterly in the south.

text.p: Isolated showers about southern coastal districts, contracting to southern coasts in the evening. Areas of morning fog inland and patchy frost about parts of the Flinders, Mid North and Murray districts. Maximum temperatures below average. West to northwesterly winds in the south turning west to southwesterly. Southeast to northeasterly winds in the north.
Isolated showers near southern coasts. Areas of morning fog and patchy frost about central and eastern districts. Maximum temperatures below average. Southeast to northeasterly winds.

Cumulative rainfall totals to the end of Wednesday: less than 2 mm about the south of the pastoral districts, 1-5 mm for the Flinders and West Coast districts and the Agricultural Areas. Isolated totals of 5-15 mm are possible about the southern coasts and ranges.
